title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that Chinese steel fasteners harm domestic producers, triggering new tariffs that increase costs for Canadian manufacturers using these parts.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imports of certain products are causing injury to Canadian manufacturers, likely triggering protective tariffs or quotas that will reduce foreign competition and support domestic producers.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of frozen strawberries from Egypt can now resume shipments after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal lifted its anti-dumping duty, removing a financial penalty on these purchases.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of wooden bedroom furniture now face a 25% import tariff after the CITT confirmed dumping by foreign competitors was harming the domestic industry.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian companies importing or selling polyester staple fibre now face a 25% tariff after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found domestic producers were harmed by cheap imports.

title: Reasons for finding — May 19, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of steel and aluminum products face new safeguard tariffs of 25% on U.S. exports, effective immediately, after the CITT confirmed injury to domestic producers from import surges.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canada's international trade tribunal found that dumped steel imports are harming local producers, so new import duties will now protect Canadian steel makers from unfair pricing.
